title: Market Brief — Monday, April 13, 2026
summary: Compute is scarce across all GPU classes. H100 availability sits at 19% (118/323), H200 at 22% (59/341), and B200 at 23% (89/380), all below the 25% scarcity threshold. H100 spot jumped 11.2% day-over-day to $1.78/hr while B200 surged 8.6% to $4.75/hr, confirming tightening supply against steady demand. B200 volatility at 96.7% annualized signals a market still discovering price.
paragraphs: Compute is scarce across all GPU classes. H100 availability sits at 19% (118/323), H200 at 22% (59/341), and B200 at 23% (89/380), all below the 25% scarcity threshold. H100 spot jumped 11.2% day-over-day to $1.78/hr while B200 surged 8.6% to $4.75/hr, confirming tightening supply against steady demand. B200 volatility at 96.7% annualized signals a market still discovering price.

TCO-adjusted inference cost on Llama 70B runs $6.51/M on H100, $6.19/M on H200, and $4.34/M on B200. At medium-tier pricing, every GPU class loses money, with H100 operators bleeding roughly $3.51/M tokens. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M output is selling at a staggering loss against any self-hosted baseline. Grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

B200 inference at $4.34/M TCO-adjusted is overvalued versus its 96.7% annualized volatility, meaning cost basis is unstable and margins are illusory for new entrants. Gemini 2.5 Flash at $2.50/M output is undervalued given Google's captive compute advantage, making it the best risk-adjusted token buy in the stack.
bullets: 19% of H100 GPUs are available, firmly in scarce territory with only 118 of 323 units free.
59 of 341 H200 GPUs are free, putting availability at 22% and nearing severe shortage levels.
$1.78/hr H100 spot is up 11.2% in a single day, the sharpest move across all classes.
96.7% annualized B200 price volatility makes it the riskiest compute bet on the board.
$0.38/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 implies a per-token loss exceeding $4/M against TCO-adjusted H100 costs.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means token prices have fallen roughly 79% since GPT-4 launch.
